violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025
Soup was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025

The following equipment was observed in poor repair -Kitchen hand sink hot water handle -Ice machine lid -1 door reach-in freezer handle -2 door slider handle Equipment must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The cutting boards in the front prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Wood is used in the construction of storage space in the walk-in cooler area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. -Speed racks in 3 door reach-in -3 door reach-in #3 shelves -Ice machine -Basement prep table Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025
The bar 3-bay sink was observed leaking. Per PIC, a new bar 3-bay sink was purchased and being delivered. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
The left chest freezer in the basement was observed with an accumulation of ice build-up. Establishment is to run a defrost on unit. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025
The floor by the ice machine area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. -Floor at bar -Floor under pizza prep table in basement (droppings) -Floor in dry storage (droppings) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitation. Establishment is to continue to work with Pest Control. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025

Employee was observed chewing gum while doing food preparation. PIC informed that eating, drinking, or chewing gum is not permitted during food preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Inspector observed an employee rinsing a knife and pizza cutter in the handwashing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed and redirected employee. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (prep table) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Cheese was observed held at 64 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, cheese had been out for less than one hour during pizza preparation. Cheese was relocated to prep unit at time of inspection. PIC informed that TCS foods must not be kept out of temperature control. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings (basement & kitchen). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Pizza prep table located in the main kitchen area was not in good repair. Inspector observed gap, creating a surface that cannot be properly cleaned and sanitized. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of: 1) Basement pizza prep (droppings) 2) Basement dry storage (droppings) 3) Pizza shelving - main kitchen (droppings, food residue) 4) Top of mixer (droppings) 5) Speed racks (food residue) 6) Shelving throughout kitchen (food residue) New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

The floor in the basement (grease trap) area is in poor repair. Inspector observed missing tile with exposed dirt. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
Tomato sauce was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The cutting boards on pizza prep units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
Freezer door handle was not in good repair, observed being held together with tape. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the dry storage shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

Mushrooms, olives and cheese were observed held at 45-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, the following food items were just rotated. PIC moved to another refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
Tomato sauce was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/18/2024

At time of inspection, ambient of pizza prep unit was 54 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC took unit out of service and all foods were moved to other pizza prep unit. Coils were cleaned at time of inspection and unit was 40 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

The cutting boards in the pizza area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
Freezer (1 door) located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, observed handle being held together with tape. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of: 1. dry storage shelving (rodent droppings) 2. equipment shelving (rodent droppings) 3. pizza pan prep shelving (food residue/rodent droppings) 4. underneath slicer (food residue) 5. refrigeration shelving throughout (food residue/other debris) 6. fans/vents (dust/dirt) 7. refrigeration gaskets (food residue/other debris) 8. ware washing area (dirt/other debris) New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024
